engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Captur Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Captur in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Captur K9K engine - diesel 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow after 80k miles, causing power loss.
DPF blockage - short-journey driving leads to regeneration failure on high-mileage units.
HPFP wear - high-pressure fuel pump degradation causes hard starting after 100k miles.

cards.altText: Renault Captur H5Ft engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure - plastic arm breaks, causing rattle and potential engine damage after 70k miles.
Turbocharger oil starvation - inadequate cooling leads to premature turbo failure on stop-start driven units.
Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear after 90k miles causes blue smoke and top-ups.

cards.altText: Renault Captur H5H engine - petrol 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator sticking - causes reduced boost and limp mode after 85k miles.
Intercooler pipe splitting - heat cycling cracks plastic connections on high-mileage Phase 2 units.
Carbon buildup on intake valves - direct injection design requires walnut blasting after 100k miles.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Captur Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level 90hp dCi powering early Captur Expression and Play trims; robust but EGR-prone.
compatibleVariants: Play dCi, Expression dCi, Expression+ dCi
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging after 80k miles causing rough idle and power loss
DPF regeneration failure on short-journey vehicles
High-pressure fuel pump wear leading to hard starting

compatibleVariants: Play dCi, Expression dCi, Dynamique dCi
commonFailures: Turbocharger actuator sticking after 90k miles
Injector seal leakage causing diesel smell in cabin
Timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage examples

Oil pump wear leading to low oil pressure warnings

Carbon buildup on intake valves affecting idle quality

Turbocharger oil starvation causing bearing failure
Piston ring wear leading to increased oil consumption after 90k miles

commonProblems.h2: Renault Captur Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Captur's 1.2L TCe and 1.5L dCi engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with timing-chain and EGR failures the most common catalysts. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Captur.

problems.h4: Renault Captur Timing Chain Failure - Why 1.2L TCe Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Renault Captur Expression+ TCe 120, Dynamique TCe 120, Iconic TCe 120 (2013-2019, H5Ft 412)
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain tensioner arm fractures under heat cycling, allowing chain stretch and potential valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, chain before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents engine damage if done before rattle appears

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, machine surface
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable if chain jump caused minor valve contact; not viable if block damaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt H5Ft engine with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for engines with internal damage; addresses root cause with improved components

problems.recommendation: On a Renault Captur worth £4,000-£8,000, preventative chain kit replacement at 60k miles offers excellent value. If you hear rattle or have misfire codes, replacement with a rebuilt unit including upgraded tensioner is more cost-effective than intermediate repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt H5Ft engine prices for your Renault Captur

problems.h4: Renault Captur EGR Valve Clogging - Why 1.5L dCi Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Renault Captur Play dCi, Expression dCi, Dynamique dCi, Iconic dCi (2013-2024, K9K series)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve accumulates carbon deposits, restricting flow and triggering limp mode or poor idle.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean valve or fit blanking plate (where legal)
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; suitable for older high-mileage Capturs not requiring MOT emissions compliance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new OEM-spec EGR valve with updated gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for Capturs under 100k miles with otherwise healthy engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.5L dCi with upgraded EGR components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for engines with secondary issues (turbo, injectors) or over 120k miles

problems.recommendation: For a Renault Captur with isolated EGR issues under 90k miles, valve replacement offers good value. If your dCi also shows turbo wear, injector faults, or DPF problems, a rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components is more economical long-term.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K Renault Captur diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Renault Captur Turbocharger Failure - TCe & dCi Engines Affected
problems.affectedModels: Renault Captur all TCe petrol (H4Bt, H5Ft, H5H) and dCi diesel (K9K) variants, 2013-2024
problems.rootCause: Oil starvation or coking in turbo bearings due to infrequent oil changes or stop-start driving patterns.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator or vacuum solenoid only
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if turbo core is healthy and failure is electronic/control-related

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned turbocharger with oil feed upgrade
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for engines under 100k miles with no other internal wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine including upgraded turbo, improved oil cooling, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Capturs or where turbo failure caused secondary engine damage

problems.recommendation: If your Renault Captur's turbo failed without warning signs and the engine is otherwise healthy under 90k miles, standalone turbo replacement is cost-effective. For engines with oil consumption, rattles, or over 100k miles, a rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo and cooling addresses the root cause.
problems.cta: Compare Renault Captur turbo replacement vs rebuilt engine costs

problems.h4: Renault Captur DPF Blockage - Diesel-Specific Regeneration Failure
problems.affectedModels: Renault Captur all K9K 1.5L dCi variants with DPF (2015-2024)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents active DPF regeneration; soot accumulation triggers pressure faults and limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic-led regeneration cycle or off-vehicle DPF cleaning
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage blockage with no physical damage to filter

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned DPF with updated sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for Capturs with otherwise healthy engines and under 110k miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K9K with DPF Delete Option
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine; DPF delete where legal for off-road/agricultural use only
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage fleet Capturs where emissions compliance is not required

problems.recommendation: For a Renault Captur used primarily for short trips, DPF cleaning plus driving habit changes may resolve issues. If blockage is severe or the engine has other wear, a rebuilt K9K with upgraded emissions components offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Renault Captur dCi DPF repair or replacement

problems.group: Renault Captur High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure
problems.h4: Renault Captur High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - dCi Diesel Specific
problems.rootCause: Internal pump wear from fuel contamination or inadequate filtration causes low rail pressure and hard starting.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace leaking seals or faulty pressure sensor only
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if pump core tests healthy and failure is peripheral

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned high-pressure fuel pump
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for engines under 110k miles with no injector damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K9K with Upgraded HPFP
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine including upgraded HPFP, calibrated injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for engines with injector damage, metal contamination, or over 120k miles

problems.recommendation: On a Renault Captur dCi with isolated HPFP issues under 100k miles, pump replacement is economical. If metal debris has entered the fuel system or injectors show wear, a rebuilt engine with upgraded HPFP and calibrated injectors prevents repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare Renault Captur HPFP repair vs full engine replacement quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Captur
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Captur suffers timing-chain rattle, EGR clogging, turbo failure or DPF blockage, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault Captur specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide supply & fit available, you can get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Captur Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Captur and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Renault Captur Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Renault Captur in the UK is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Renault Captur 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K dCi is the backbone of the Captur diesel range, fitted across Phase 1 (2013-2019) and Phase 2 (2019-2024) models. Known for strong fuel economy but prone to EGR and DPF issues on short-journey vehicles, it powers entry-level Play through to top-spec Initiale Paris trims.
commonFailurePoints: high-pressure fuel pump wear at higher mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Captur 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Captur 0.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 0.9L H4Bt TCe three-cylinder powered early Captur Play and Expression trims (2013-2019). Economical and nimble in urban use but timing-chain sensitive; best suited to low-mileage, well-maintained examples.
items.cta: Compare Renault Captur 0.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Captur 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The modern 1.0L H4D TCe replaced the 0.9L unit in Phase 2 Captur (2019-2024), offering improved refinement and emissions. Fitted to Play and Zen trims; more robust than predecessor but still requires regular oil changes to protect turbo.
items.cta: Compare Renault Captur 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Captur 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The popular 1.2L H5Ft TCe powered mid-range Captur trims (2013-2019) with strong performance but notorious timing-chain issues in pre-2016 units. Requires vigilant maintenance; rebuilt units with upgraded tensioners are recommended for replacement.
commonFailurePoints: piston ring wear causing oil consumption
items.cta: Compare Renault Captur 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Captur 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The joint-developed 1.3L H5H TCe (with Mercedes) powers Phase 2 Captur Zen, Intens and S-Edition trims (2019-2024). More reliable than 1.2L predecessor but still susceptible to turbo wastegate and intercooler issues at higher mileage.
items.cta: Compare Renault Captur 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Captur 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 1.6L K4M was fitted to early Captur variants in limited markets; simple design but less common in UK. Reliable if maintained but lacks turbo performance; replacement demand is low.
commonFailurePoints: head gasket failure on neglected units
items.cta: Compare Renault Captur 1.6L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Renault Captur Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Renault Captur 1.6L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The E-Tech full-hybrid system pairs a 1.6L H4M petrol engine with dual electric motors and a multi-mode clutchless transmission. Fitted to Zen, Intens and Initiale Paris E-Tech trims (2020-2024); complex but efficient for urban use.
commonFailurePoints: clutch actuator wear affecting mode transitions
items.cta: Enquire about Renault Captur 1.6L hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: Renault Captur Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Renault Captur engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 0.9L TCe petrol to the latest E-Tech Hybrid.

items.title: Renault Captur Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K dCi dominates Captur diesel replacements, powering Phase 1 and Phase 2 models with strong economy but predictable EGR/DPF wear on high-mileage or short-journey vehicles.
items.descriptor: The 1.5L K9K dCi dominates Captur diesel replacements, powering Phase 1 and Phase 2 models with strong economy but predictable EGR/DPF wear on high-mileage or short-journey vehicles.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Captur Diesel Engine Families
families: K9K 608/628 - 90hp entry diesel (Play/Expression), EGR clogging common after 80k miles
K9K 856 - 110hp mid-range (Dynamique/Iconic), HPFP wear at higher mileage
K9K 872 - 115hp Blue dCi (Phase 2), AdBlue injector faults on Euro 6 units
knownFor: Excellent motorway fuel economy (60+ mpg achievable)
EGR valve clogging causing power loss on short-journey cars
DPF regeneration failure requiring forced cleaning or replacement
High UK replacement demand on Dynamique and Iconic trims
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Captur Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Renault Captur Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Capturs use turbocharged TCe engines from 0.9L to 1.3L; the 1.2L H5Ft is most searched for replacement due to timing-chain issues, while newer 1.3L H5H offers improved reliability.
items.descriptor: Petrol Capturs use turbocharged TCe engines from 0.9L to 1.3L; the 1.2L H5Ft is most searched for replacement due to timing-chain issues, while newer 1.3L H5H offers improved reliability.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Captur Petrol Engine Families
families: H4Bt 444 - 0.9L TCe 90hp (early Play/Expression), timing-chain tensioner failures
H5Ft 412 - 1.2L TCe 120hp (mid-range trims), notorious pre-2016 timing-chain issues
H5H 450/490 - 1.3L TCe 130/150hp (Phase 2), turbo wastegate and intercooler concerns
H4D 450 - 1.0L TCe 100hp (Phase 2 entry), more robust but still turbo-sensitive
knownFor: Strong low-end torque from turbocharging across all sizes
Timing-chain tensioner failures on 0.9L and 1.2L pre-2016 units
Carbon buildup on direct-injection valves requiring walnut blasting
High search volume for 1.2L TCe replacement due to known failure patterns
items.cta: Compare Renault Captur Petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Captur Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: E-Tech full-hybrid Captur pairs a 1.6L petrol engine with dual electric motors for urban efficiency; specialist knowledge required for replacement due to complex powertrain integration.
items.descriptor: E-Tech full-hybrid Captur pairs a 1.6L petrol engine with dual electric motors for urban efficiency; specialist knowledge required for replacement due to complex powertrain integration.
families: 48-volt starter-generator recovers braking energy for electric assist
Multi-mode clutchless transmission enables seamless EV/petrol transitions
Battery health check recommended during any engine replacement procedure
foundIn: E-Tech Hybrid Zen, Intens, Initiale Paris (2020-2024)
importantNotes: Hybrid system requires specialist diagnostics and coding post-fit
Battery and inverter inspection recommended when replacing petrol engine
Availability of rebuilt hybrid powertrains is more limited than standard engines

engineYears.h2: Renault Captur Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault Captur's UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Phase 1 Launch: Early TCe & dCi Engines
years.description: The Captur launched with 0.9L/1.2L TCe petrol and 1.5L dCi diesel engines; timing-chain issues on early 1.2L units dominate replacement enquiries from this period.
keyChanges: UK launch of Renault Captur with Expression, Dynamique and Iconic trims
Introduction of 0.9L H4Bt and 1.2L H5Ft TCe petrol engines
1.5L K9K dCi diesel offered with 90hp output across core trims
knownFor: 1.2L TCe timing-chain tensioner failures after 60k miles
Strong fuel economy from 1.5L dCi on motorway-driven examples
EGR clogging on diesel Capturs used for short urban journeys
High search volume for H5Ft 412 replacement engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2016 Renault Captur replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Captur 1.2 TCe timing chain replacement • Captur 1.5 dCi EGR valve fix • H5Ft 412 engine replacement cost
enquiries: Renault Captur 1.2 TCe timing chain replacement

years.preview: Phase 1 Facelift: Refined Engines & New Trims
years.description: The 2017 facelift introduced revised engine calibrations and the 110hp K9K 856 diesel; timing-chain updates on later 1.2L TCe units improved reliability.
keyChanges: Facelifted exterior and interior with updated infotainment
Introduction of 110hp K9K 856 diesel for Dynamique/Iconic trims
Revised H5Ft 412 calibration on late-production 1.2L TCe units
mainEngines: H4Bt 444 - 0.9L petrol TCe 90hp (entry trims, phased out late 2019)
knownFor: Improved timing-chain durability on post-2017 1.2L TCe units
DPF regeneration issues on diesel Capturs with high urban mileage
Strong residual values for well-maintained Iconic and S-Edition trims
Continued high demand for K9K 856 rebuilt engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Renault Captur replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Captur K9K 856 engine replacement • Captur 1.2 TCe turbo failure fix • Iconic dCi DPF regeneration cost

years.preview: Phase 2 Launch: New Platform & Engine Families
years.description: The all-new Phase 2 Captur on CMF-B platform introduced 1.0L and 1.3L TCe petrol engines plus E-Tech hybrid; K9K 872 Blue dCi diesel met Euro 6 emissions.
keyChanges: New CMF-B platform shared with Clio V and Nissan Juke
Introduction of 1.0L H4D and 1.3L H5H TCe petrol engines
Launch of E-Tech full-hybrid powertrain with 1.6L H4M base engine
K9K 872 Blue dCi diesel with AdBlue for Euro 6 compliance
mainEngines: H5H 450/490 - 1.3L petrol TCe 130/150hp (Zen, Intens, S-Edition, Initiale Paris)
K9K 872 - 1.5L diesel Blue dCi 115hp (Zen, Intens, Initiale Paris)
H4M 450 - 1.6L petrol hybrid base (E-Tech Hybrid trims)
knownFor: Improved refinement and emissions across all Phase 2 engines
Turbo wastegate issues on early 1.3L H5H TCe units after 80k miles
Hybrid system complexity requiring specialist knowledge for repairs
Strong demand for K9K 872 rebuilt engines from fleet users
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2021 Renault Captur replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Captur 1.3 TCe turbo replacement • Captur E-Tech hybrid battery check • K9K 872 Blue dCi AdBlue fault fix
enquiries: K9K 872 Blue dCi AdBlue fault fix

years.description: Late Phase 2 Captur saw hybrid range expansion and minor engine calibrations; replacement demand focuses on 1.3L TCe turbo and K9K 872 EGR components.
keyChanges: Expansion of E-Tech Hybrid to more trim levels
Minor ECU updates for improved emissions compliance
Introduction of Initiale Paris as flagship trim with 1.3L TCe 150hp
mainEngines: K9K 872 - 1.5L diesel Blue dCi 115hp (all diesel trims)
H4M 450 - 1.6L petrol hybrid (E-Tech Hybrid Zen/Intens/Initiale Paris)
knownFor: Strong reliability on well-maintained 1.3L TCe units with regular oil changes
AdBlue injector faults on K9K 872 Blue dCi after 90k miles
Hybrid battery cooling system maintenance critical for long-term E-Tech reliability
High-value rebuilt engines available for Phase 2 variants with upgraded components

faq.h2: Renault Captur Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault Captur engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices for Renault Captur range from £1,200 for entry 0.9L TCe units to £2,600 for 1.3L TCe or 1.5L dCi, with hybrid E-Tech systems costing £2,800-£4,200 due to complexity.
keyPoints: Prices are supply-only; add £400-£800 for supply & fit labour
Used engines start around £800 but carry higher risk
Rebuilt units include 12-month warranty from UK specialists
Exact cost depends on engine code, variant and supplier location
items.cta: Compare Renault Captur engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Renault Captur?
items.answer: Common Renault Captur engine codes include K9K (1.5L dCi diesel), H5Ft/H5H (1.2L/1.3L TCe petrol), H4Bt (0.9L TCe) and H4M (1.6L E-Tech hybrid); verify via DVLA lookup or engine bay sticker.
keyPoints: Hybrid: H4M 450 (1.6L E-Tech system)
Enter registration above for instant code verification
items.cta: Check your Renault Captur engine code compatibility now

items.question: Do you supply and fit Renault Captur engines?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists offering Renault Captur engine supply & fit services nationwide, including labour, fluids, ECU coding and post-fit diagnostics.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £400-£800 to engine price depending on region
All fitted engines include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Mobile fitting available in many areas for added convenience
Book online after comparing quotes from vetted specialists
items.cta: Book your Renault Captur engine supply & fit service today

items.question: What warranty comes with a Renault Captur recon engine?
items.answer: Reconditioned and rebuilt Renault Captur engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering internal components and assembly defects.
keyPoints: Warranty valid at any UK garage for claims processing
Excludes normal wear items and misuse damage
Some suppliers offer extended 18-24 month options
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: View Renault Captur recon engine warranty options

items.question: How long does Renault Captur engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical Renault Captur engine replacement takes 6-8 hours labour plus 1-3 days for engine sourcing; supply & fit bookings can often be completed within a week from quote acceptance.
keyPoints: Engine sourcing: 1-3 days depending on code availability
Labour time: 6-8 hours for experienced Renault specialists
Post-fit diagnostics and road test add 1-2 hours
Express next-day engine delivery available for common codes
items.cta: Get fast quotes for Renault Captur engine replacement

items.question: Can I replace my Captur engine with a different code?
items.answer: Engine code swaps on Renault Captur are possible but require ECU reprogramming, mounting verification and emissions compliance checks; OEM-matched codes are strongly recommended for reliability and legality.
keyPoints: Same-displacement codes (e.g., K9K 608 to 856) often compatible
ECU coding required for power/output differences
Emissions compliance must be maintained for MOT legality
Consult a Renault specialist before proceeding with any swap
items.cta: Speak to a Renault Captur engine compatibility expert

items.question: Are used Renault Captur engines reliable?
items.answer: Used Renault Captur engines can be reliable if sourced from low-mileage, accident-damaged donors with full service history; always request compression test results and mileage verification before purchase.
keyPoints: Prefer engines under 80k miles with documented maintenance
Request compression test and leak-down report pre-purchase
Avoid engines from vehicles with known timing-chain or turbo failures
Short-term warranty (3-6 months) recommended for used units
items.cta: Check used Renault Captur engine availability with verification

items.question: What is the difference between recon and remanufactured Captur engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Renault Captur engines replace worn parts to restore function; remanufactured units renew every component to factory specs, offering closer-to-new reliability at higher cost.
keyPoints: Recon: Cost-effective, addresses known failure points
Reman: Comprehensive renewal, ideal for newer or high-value Capturs
Both include 12-month warranty from UK specialists
Reman units often include extended warranty options
items.cta: Compare recon vs remanufactured Renault Captur engine costs

items.question: Do Renault Captur hybrid engines cost more to replace?
items.answer: Yes, Renault Captur E-Tech hybrid engine replacement costs £2,800-£4,200 due to complex powertrain integration, specialist diagnostics and limited supplier availability versus standard petrol/diesel units.
keyPoints: Hybrid systems require battery/inverter inspection during engine work
Specialist coding and calibration needed post-fit
Fewer UK suppliers offer rebuilt hybrid powertrains
Budget extra time for sourcing and fitting hybrid units
items.cta: Enquire about Renault Captur E-Tech hybrid engine availability

items.question: How do I find my Renault Captur engine code?
items.answer: Your Renault Captur engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the gearbox), listed on the V5C logbook, or verifiable instantly by entering your registration number above for DVLA lookup.
keyPoints: Common locations: engine block sticker, V5C section D, service records
Codes start with K9K (diesel), H4Bt/H5Ft/H5H (petrol), H4M (hybrid)
DVLA lookup via registration is fastest and most accurate
Engine code essential for ordering correct replacement unit
items.cta: Use our free Renault Captur engine code checker now
